pullRequest.title: Update activity parameter node name and types in Model browser
pullRequest.body:
This feature builds on #3827.

### PR Type
What kind of change does this PR introduce?

<!-- Please check the one that applies to this PR using "x". -->
- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Chore (refactoring, formatting, local variables, other cleanup)
- [ ] Documentation content changes

### What is the current behavior?

Related names in the model browser are not updated.

Issue Number: Fixes #3791, #2852. Closes #3827.

### What is the new behavior?

Synchronize related elements in the Model browser, so everything looks up to date.

I took @mvinca's PR and simplified it a bit. It now works for ActivityParameterNode's and Properties (any Typed Element).

This makes the UI behave more consistently. If I missed a case, I think it's best to just add it to `TreeModel.sync()`.

### Does this PR introduce a breaking change?
- [ ] Yes
- [x] No

<!-- If this PR contains a breaking change, please describe the impact and migration path for existing applications below. -->


### Other information

Thanks again @mvinca for your work. It helped a lot in shaping this solution.

pullRequest.title: Fix build flakiness with Read the Docs
pullRequest.body:
Closes #3856. Our dependencies were pretty out of date due to a delay in us upgrading to PyGObject. On ReadTheDocs, we had virtualenv pinned to an older version than what the runner had. This was causing it to downgrade during the middle of dependency installation, which resulted in a file not found error.

This PR pins PyGObject < 3.52 (to be updated in #3797) and then updates all the other dependencies to fix the issue.

pullRequest.title: Windows: Set English as the default installer language
pullRequest.body:
Fixes #3901 by moving English to the top language macro which makes it the default. I also added all the other languages available by default with NSIS. I was thinking even if Gaphor itself is not translated to all of those, it would be nice having the installer in the native GUI language.

### PR Checklist
Please check if your PR fulfills the following requirements:

- [X] I have read, and I am following the [Contributor guide](https://github.com/gaphor/gaphor/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)
- [X] I have read, and I understand the GNOME [Code of Conduct](https://conduct.gnome.org/)

### PR Type
What kind of change does this PR introduce?

<!-- Please check the one that applies to this PR using "x". -->
- [X] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Chore (refactoring, formatting, local variables, other cleanup)
- [ ] Documentation content changes

### What is the current behavior?
If a user is using a language that is not part of the 17, they get the first language which is Catalan. In #3901 they reported it as French - so not sure if that is accurate or not, but at least they didn't get the GUI language or English as a backup.

Issue Number: N/A

### What is the new behavior?
Added more languages, with English being the first and default.

### Does this PR introduce a breaking change?
- [ ] Yes
- [X] No

<!-- If this PR contains a breaking change, please describe the impact and migration path for existing applications below. -->


### Other information

pullRequest.title: Convert CI from mingw to gvsbuild (Sourcery refactored)
pullRequest.body:
Pull Request #912 refactored by [Sourcery](https://sourcery.ai/github/). If you're happy with these changes, merge this Pull Request using the *Squash and merge* strategy. **NOTE**: As code is pushed to the original Pull Request, Sourcery will re-run and update (force-push) this Pull Request with new refactorings as necessary. If Sourcery finds no refactorings at any point, this Pull Request will be closed automatically. See our documentation [here](https://github.com/sourcery-ai/sourcery/wiki/GitHub-App-Usage).
Run Sourcery locally

Reduce the feedback loop during development by using the Sourcery editor plugin:

  • VS Code
  • PyCharm
Review changes via command line

To manually merge these changes, make sure you're on the chore/mingw-to-gvsbuild branch, then run:

git fetch origin sourcery/chore/mingw-to-gvsbuild
git merge --ff-only FETCH_HEAD
git reset HEAD^
Help us [improve](https://research.typeform.com/to/j06Spdfr?type=pr_refactor&github_login=danyeaw&base_repo=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fgaphor%2Fgaphor.git&base_remote_ref=refs%2Fpull%2F912%2Fhead&base_ref=chore%2Fmingw-to-gvsbuild&base_sha=7e8c48bf28f238c012f7b13449faba6e0a3c58a5&head_repo=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fgaphor%2Fgaphor.git&head_ref=sourcery%2Fchore%2Fmingw-to-gvsbuild&base_pr_number=912&base_pr_state=open) this pull request!

pullRequest.title: Stereotype updates for interfaces and operations
pullRequest.body:
### PR Type
What kind of change does this PR introduce?

<!-- Please check the one that applies to this PR using "x". -->
- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Chore (refactoring, formatting, local variables, other cleanup)
- [ ] Documentation content changes

### What is the current behavior?
<!-- Please describe the current behavior that you are modifying, or link to a relevant issue. -->

Issue Number: Fixes #4130, #4129, #4165

### What is the new behavior?

* When an interface is assigned a stereotype, the "interface" label is no longer shown
* When an operation is assigned a stereotype, it shows in the diagram
* Element editor is no longer updated when hovering over an element. You have to explicitly select it.
* Fix warning about non-escaped text in element editor.

### Does this PR introduce a breaking change?
- [ ] Yes
- [x] No

<!-- If this PR contains a breaking change, please describe the impact and migration path for existing applications below. -->

pullRequest.body: This prevents small diagrams from being blown up. This change only affects the default zoom level.
